prompts.name: GNHF Agent Spawn Readiness Preflight
prompts.sprintPathRole: Prove the selected agent can be spawned before a long run
prompts.useThisWhen: Before using a new agent, shell domain, workstation, or GNHF adapter overnight.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: The exact agent command has already passed a bounded child-process spawn test in the current shell.
prompts.inspectFirst: Repo cleanliness, gnhf command, exact agent command path, native versus ACP form, bounded child-process probe.
prompts.expectedOutput: A spawnability result with exact command path, exit code, and actionable blocker before GNHF opens.
prompts.nextStep: Use P38 only after PASS; repair the adapter or choose another proven agent after FAIL.
prompts.acceptanceGate: The same shell can spawn the exact command without timeout; command presence alone is not treated as readiness.
prompts.authority: Read-only shell and repository readiness authority
prompts.proofCeiling: Spawn and path proof only; no hosted inference or repository mutation proof.

prompts.name: GNHF Night Queue Compiler
prompts.sprintPathRole: Compile and commit a finite overnight repair queue
prompts.useThisWhen: A repo such as AxTask or BlacksmithGuild has many issues and the next repair set must be bounded before sleep.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: A current committed queue already contains ready items with exact acceptance commands.
prompts.inspectFirst: Repo rules, canonical tests, issue sources, TODOs, recent commits, existing plans, and owned paths.
prompts.expectedOutput: Committed queue and baseline report containing no more than five evidence-backed repair items.
prompts.nextStep: Use P39 for general items, P40 for a failure cluster, or P41 for a stateful app/game.
prompts.acceptanceGate: Every item has evidence, owned paths, dependencies, an exact validation command, and a ready or blocked state.
prompts.authority: Queue and report files only; no product-code repair
prompts.proofCeiling: Planning artifact commit only; no repair or runtime proof.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent "xyz_gnhf_agent_spec" `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 2 `
--max-tokens 180000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "The finite night queue and baseline report are committed, every item has an exact acceptance command, and the worktree is clean." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: Night queue compiler
Lane: issue-heavy repo planning with a committed local deliverable

Prerequisite:
- P37 passed for this exact agent specification and shell domain.

Issue sources:
- xyz_issue_sources

Tracked outputs:
- Queue: xyz_night_queue_path
- Report: xyz_night_report_path

Objective:
Inspect current repository evidence and compile a finite queue of no more than five repair items. Commit the queue and baseline report. Do not repair product code in this run.

Each item must include:
- stable ID and concise title
- evidence path, issue, failing command, or reproducible symptom
- root-cause hypothesis labeled as hypothesis
- owned files or directories
- forbidden surfaces
- dependency order
- exact targeted validation command
- estimated risk and size
- state: ready or blocked

Rules:
- Read AGENTS.md and applicable repo rules first.
- Prefer current code, tests, and recent history over stale plans.
- Use read-only GitHub issue queries only when authenticated and useful.
- Do not add speculative ideas merely to fill the queue.
- No remote mutation, branch deletion, push, merge, release, credential work, or live personal-state mutation.
- A queue/report commit is mandatory even when every candidate is blocked.

Commit:
- Commit only the queue, report, and directly required parent directories.
- Suggested message: docs(ops): compile bounded night repair queue

Report:
- repo floor and canonical validation
- candidate ledger and why each item was selected or rejected
- ready queue in dependency order
- blocked items
- exact next prompt: P39, P40, or P41
- commit SHA
- git status"

prompts.name: GNHF Overnight Repair Batch
prompts.sprintPathRole: Execute a committed queue with checkpoint commits
prompts.useThisWhen: P37 passed and P38 committed a finite queue with ready local code repairs.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: The work is remote-only, open-ended, lacks exact validation, or requires live interaction.
prompts.inspectFirst: Committed queue, repo rules, exact acceptance commands, current branch/worktree, and canonical proof.
prompts.expectedOutput: Up to three repaired queue items, one coherent commit per item, and an updated committed night report.
prompts.nextStep: Use P42 to refill, P43 after interruption, or P44 in the morning.
prompts.acceptanceGate: No repeated identical failure, no remote-only work, every attempted item is committed or blocked with evidence.
prompts.authority: Local code, tests, queue, and report within supplied scope
prompts.proofCeiling: Committed local repair proof only; no remote integration or live behavior claim.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent "xyz_gnhf_agent_spec" `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 8 `
--max-tokens 800000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "Up to three ready queue items are committed or blocked with evidence, the queue and night report are current, and the worktree is clean." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: Overnight repair batch
Lane: checkpointed local code repair

Prerequisites:
- P37 passed for this exact agent.
- xyz_night_queue_path exists in the starting commit.
- The repository is clean and on an attached non-GNHF base branch.

Inputs:
- Queue: xyz_night_queue_path
- Report: xyz_night_report_path
- Canonical proof: xyz_canonical_proof

Objective:
Select at most three ready queue items in dependency order. Work on one item at a time. Produce one coherent commit per completed item and update the queue and report inside that same commit.

Per-item loop:
1. Reconfirm evidence, owned scope, and exact acceptance command.
2. Reproduce the defect or prove the missing contract.
3. Implement the smallest correct repair.
4. Run targeted validation.
5. Run xyz_canonical_proof after shared contract changes.
6. Update the item disposition and checkpoint report.
7. Commit before starting another item.

No-progress rules:
- Do not retry an identical command failure more than once without a code, configuration, or evidence change.
- Stop an item after two bounded repair attempts.
- Stop the whole run after two consecutive iterations with no tracked diff.
- Authentication, quota, spawn, network, and unknown partial-state failures stop the run; they do not authorize blind failover.
- Bound every wait and test command.

Forbidden:
- remote-only PR cleanup or comment work
- adding unrelated backlog
- push, merge, release, force-push, credentials, deployment, live targets, destructive Git
- hiding failures with skips, log suppression, or inflated timeouts

Final report:
- items attempted and dispositions
- commits in order
- tests and exact results
- blockers and preserved state
- remaining ready queue
- next prompt: P42, P43, or P44
- proof ceiling
- git status"
